name: Forest Essentials Hair Cleanser, Bhringraj & Shikakai
short_reason: Ayurvedic, sulphate-free cleanser aimed at reducing hair fall and supporting scalp health without harsh stripping.
why_recommended: Formulated with traditional Ayurvedic herbs like bhringraj and shikakai.
SLS/SLES free and gentle for regular cleansing without dryness. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
Supports reduction in hair thinning, breakage and promotes scalp nourishment. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}
ingredient_summary: Shikakai, Bhringraj, Reetha, Coconut Milk, Hydrolyzed Wheat Protein, Licorice, Kalonji Seed Oil

name: 8-in-1 Root Nourishing Amla & Neem Shampoo
short_reason: Herbal, ingredient-transparent anti-hairfall/dandruff shampoo (amla + neem) that is SLS/SLES- and paraben-free and uses mild surfactants—suitable as a safe starting point.
why_recommended: Brand and product pages list active botanicals (Amla, Neem, Hibiscus, Henna, Fenugreek, Vetiver, Wheatgerm oil, Soy Lecithin).
Explicit claims and listings show the product is free from SLS/SLES and parabens (reduces stripping and long-term scalp risk).
Formulation is plant-forward and positioned for routine use for oily/dandruff-prone scalps.
pros: Relevant herbal actives for dandruff/hair fall
cons: May include mild synthetic emulsifiers (standard on mid-range herbals)
ingredient_summary: Amla (Emblica officinalis), Hibiscus, Henna (Lawsonia inermis), Fenugreek (Trigonella), Neem (Azadirachta indica), Vetiver, Wheatgerm Oil, Soy Lecithin; water, mild surfactant system and mild preservatives (per brand/retailer ingredient listings).

name: SoulTree Licorice (Mulethi) Hair Repair Shampoo with Strengthening Bhringraj
short_reason: BDIH-certified, herb-forward shampoo using mild, low-irritation surfactants (coco/decyl glucosides, sodium cocoyl glutamate) and preservative systems that avoid sulfates, parabens and harsh formaldehyde releasers.
why_recommended: Full ingredient panel published (no hidden ‘base q.s.’).
Cleansing uses mild, low-irritation surfactants (Coco glucoside, Lauryl glucoside, Sodium cocoyl glutamate) rather than SLS/SLES.
Contains functional Ayurvedic botanicals (Glycyrrhiza glabra — Licorice, Eclipta alba — Bhringraj, Shikakai, Amla, Hibiscus, Henna) that support scalp health and hair strength.
Certified ‘Natural’ under BDIH (European natural cosmetics standard) — a strong trust signal for formulation and ingredient sourcing.
pros: Transparent INCI / ingredients on product page
Mild surfactants and mild preservative system (sodium benzoate, potassium sorbate)
cons: Contains perfume (fragrance) with Linalool/Limonene — potential irritant for very sensitive scalps
ingredient_summary: Key ingredients: Aqua; Coco-glucoside; Lauryl glucoside; Sodium cocoyl glutamate; Glyceryl oleate; Betaine; Glycerine; Coco-caprylate/caprate; Caprylyl/Capryl glucoside; Glycyrrhiza glabra (Licorice)*; Eclipta alba (Bhringraj)*; Acacia concinna (Shikakai)*; Emblica officinalis (Amla)*; Hibiscus rosa-sinensis*; Lawsonia inermis (Henna)*; Aloe barbadensis*; Hydrolysed wheat protein; Preservatives: Sodium benzoate, Potassium sorbate; Perfume (contains Linalool, Limonene).

name: Bringadi Hair Cleanser (Bringadi Nourishing Shampoo)
short_reason: 96% natural-origin, herb-forward cleanser using mild, low-irritation surfactants and published INCI — suitable for long-term scalp health and for coloured/chemically treated hair.
why_recommended: Full INCI published; surfactants are mild glucosides and cocoyl glutamate rather than sulfates.
Contains functional Ayurvedic actives (Bhringraj, Amla, Indigo, Licorice, Shikakai) with evidence/use for scalp health and hair strength.
Brand claims clinical benefit on sebum reduction and hair-fall metrics; product marketed for treated/coloured hair (lower risk of stripping).
pros: Ayurvedic actives with supporting formulation logic
cons: Contains fragrance (may include fragrance allergens) — potential for very sensitive scalps
ingredient_summary: Key INCI highlights: Aqua; Lauryl Glucoside; Disodium Cocoyl Glutamate; Sodium Cocoamphoacetate; Glycerin; Betaine; Glyceryl Oleate; Hydrolysed Soy Protein; D-Panthenol; Botanical extracts — Eclipta alba (Bhringraj), Emblica officinalis (Amla), Indigofera tinctoria (Indigo), Glycyrrhiza glabra (Licorice), Acacia concinna (Shikakai), Hibiscus rosa-sinensis; Preservatives: Sodium Benzoate, Potassium Sorbate; Buffer: Citric Acid; Perfume (may list Linalool/Limonene).

name: Goat Milk Shampoo (with Ceramides & Pea Protein)
short_reason: pH-balanced, sulphate-free, goat-milk shampoo with ceramides and pea protein — gentle surfactant system and published INCI make it a safe starting point for dry, frizzy, or fragile hair.
why_recommended: Brand publishes ingredient panel and markets the product as pH-balanced and sulphate-free (transparent).
Key functional actives — Goat Milk, Ceramides, Pea Protein — support moisture, barrier repair and reduced breakage.
Uses mild surfactants (cocamidopropyl hydroxysultaine, cocamidopropyl betaine, coco-glucoside) rather than SLS/SLES.
pros: Functional actives for moisturizing & frizz control
cons: Contains fragrance (possible allergen for very sensitive scalps)
Not positioned as anti-dandruff — limited efficacy for severe fungal dandruff
ingredient_summary: Aqua; Goat Milk (farm fresh); Jasmine Hydrosol; Cocamidopropyl Hydroxysultaine; Cocamidopropyl Betaine; Coco-Glucoside; Glycerin; Pea Protein; Ceramides; Hydrolysed Wheat Protein; Botanical extracts; Preservative: Sodium Benzoate / Potassium Sorbate (per product label images).

name: Arata Super Shampoo (Onion + Argan + Apple Cider Vinegar)
short_reason: Mild, pH-friendly shampoo with onion oil, argan oil and apple-cider vinegar; published INCI shows low-irritation surfactants and mild preservatives, making it a safe starting point for hair-fall and scalp balance.
why_recommended: Full ingredient list published on the brand page (transparent INCI).
Uses gentle surfactants (Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate, Sodium Cocoyl Glycinate, Cocamidopropyl Betaine) instead of SLS/SLES.
Contains functional actives relevant to hair fall and scalp microbiome balance (Onion oil, Argan oil, Apple Cider Vinegar).
pros: Functional botanicals and vinegar for pH/scalp support
cons: Contains fragrance; may have fragrance allergens for very sensitive scalps
Not ECOCERT/BDIH certified (no major natural cert shown)
ingredient_summary: Purified Water; Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate; Glycerin; Sodium Cocoyl Glycinate; Cocamidopropyl Betaine; Guar Hydroxypropyltrimonium Chloride; Aloe Vera Extract; Bhringraj (Eclipta alba) Extract; Hydrolysed Pea Protein; Apple Cider Vinegar; Argania spinosa (Argan) Oil; Caprylhydroxamic Acid; Sodium Benzoate; Onion (Allium cepa) Oil; Fragrance (contains typical fragrance allergens).

name: Organic Hair Cleanser (Powdered Shikakai, Amla & Hibiscus)
short_reason: 100% botanical powdered cleanser (Shikakai, Amla, Hibiscus) with full ingredient transparency — chemical-free and suitable as a gentle weekly detox.
why_recommended: Pure botanical powders (Shikakai, Amla, Hibiscus and similar) — no synthetic surfactants or preservatives listed.
Explicit product & brand claims: 'chemical-free', 'paraben, sulphate and SLS free' and sourcing transparency from Godavari farms.
Good match for sensitive scalps or users seeking a zero-chemical weekly cleanse.
cons: Limited efficacy for heavy/oily scalps or severe fungal dandruff
ingredient_summary: Powdered Reetha / Shikakai, Amla, Hibiscus and other dried botanical powders (handpicked organic herbs sourced from Godavari region).

name: Murumuru Butter Shampoo Bar
short_reason: Plastic-free, SCI-based shampoo bar centered on murumuru butter — gentle, pH-friendly cleansing suitable for dry or frizzy hair and for travel.
why_recommended: Typically formulated with Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate (SCI) or similar mild surfactants rather than soap or SLS, which reduces scalp irritation and works in Indian water profiles.
Murumuru butter provides conditioning lipids that help with dry, frizzy hair without leaving heavy silicone residue.
Solid bar format reduces plastic waste and is travel/convenience friendly while still offering a pH-balanced cleansing experience.
pros: Conditioning murumuru butter for dry hair
cons: Learning curve for bar use and lathering
May contain fragrance — potential allergen for ultra-sensitive scalps
ingredient_summary: Key functional ingredients (typical): Murumuru butter; mild solid surfactant (e.g. Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate); glycerin; conditioning fatty alcohols/esters; botanical oils or butters; tocopherol (vitamin E); preservative system appropriate for solids (if any); fragrance. — **Confirm exact INCI on label.**
